transformation_type: three-component coupling of carboxylic acid, carbonyl compound, and isocyanide to α-acyloxycarboxamides or related adducts
mechanism_type: multicomponent addition followed by intramolecular acyl transfer from a nitrilium/carboxylate-derived intermediate
reactant_pattern_text: carboxylic acid + aldehyde or ketone + isocyanide
common_conditions: typically run under mild one-pot conditions that combine an acid, a carbonyl partner, and an isocyanide; variants use water or HN3 to redirect product class
description_short: Classical isocyanide-based multicomponent reaction that rapidly assembles α-acyloxycarboxamides from simple acids, carbonyl compounds, and isocyanides.

transformation_type: photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition of a carbonyl compound with an alkene to form an oxetane
mechanism_type: UV-induced carbonyl excitation followed by 1,4-biradical formation and closure to oxetane
common_conditions: irradiation with UV light or sunlight; regioselectivity depends on substitution and stabilization of the intermediate biradical
description_short: Photochemical carbonyl–alkene cycloaddition that furnishes oxetanes and is widely used in mechanistic photochemistry and natural-product synthesis.

transformation_type: formal [2+2+1] cycloaddition of alkyne, alkene, and carbon monoxide to cyclopentenones
mechanism_type: cobalt-alkyne complex mediated carbonylative cyclization with alkene coordination and CO insertion
reactant_pattern_text: alkyne + alkene + CO or enyne-alkyne cobalt complex
common_conditions: typically employs dicobalt-alkyne complexes under heat or promoter-assisted conditions; intra- and intermolecular variants are common
description_short: Carbonylative cyclization that converts alkyne/alkene combinations into cyclopentenones and serves as a staple annulation in target-oriented synthesis.

transformation_type: base-catalyzed migration of a 2,3-epoxy alcohol to an isomeric epoxide; aza/thia variants form aziridino or thiirano alcohols
mechanism_type: alkoxide-initiated intramolecular nucleophilic epoxide opening and reclosure via 3-exo-tet displacement
reactant_pattern_text: 2,3-epoxy alcohol, amino alcohol, or thioacetate-derived epoxy alcohol
product_pattern_text: isomeric 2,3-epoxy alcohol, aziridino alcohol, or thiirano alcohol
common_conditions: catalytic or stoichiometric base generates an alkoxide that opens and recloses the epoxide; product distribution depends on substrate, solvent, and nucleophile trapping
description_short: Epoxide migration manifold that relays stereochemistry and functional handles across epoxy alcohols and related heteroatom-substituted systems.

transformation_type: base-promoted condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or azlactone precursors to α,β-unsaturated acids or azlactones
mechanism_type: anhydride or azlactone enolization, carbonyl addition, and elimination to the conjugated product
reactant_pattern_text: aromatic aldehyde + acid anhydride or N-acyl glycine derivative
product_pattern_text: α,β-unsaturated carboxylic acid, azlactone, or α-arylcinnamic acid derivative
common_conditions: best with aromatic aldehydes; weak base and heating promote condensation, while azlactone and mixed-anhydride variants broaden substrate classes
description_short: Classical olefination manifold that converts aromatic aldehydes into cinnamic-acid-type products or azlactone intermediates using anhydride-based carbon nucleophiles.
